comment.body:
I found it in the monthly post

> What market expects is, that volatility remains supplied/stairstep up, but this dampening effect is fading into the OpEx week, until they expect the market to range (follow weekly posts) in an expanded range, then they expect volatility squeeze on FOMC (similarly to 2024 Dec FOMC), then OpEx will provide some slowdown/downside support (due to the negative speed profile), Monday some delta buyback, and then further sell-off, but on a slowed manner, and Trump is expected to bail out the sentiment.

comment.body:
Alma, I'm grateful for your modeling and read on the market and "house". I'm newish to options. When you moved beyond BSM to add skewness and kurtosis, which model did you use? 
I think you mentioned it in a chat some time back, and you posted a paper. Is it something like:
"a non-rough path-dependent model and a two-factor Markovian model that outperform their rough counterparts in capturing SPX smiles between one week and three years, with only 3 to 4 parameters"
From: https://arxiv.org/html/2401.03345v2
Thank you.
comment.raw_body:
Alma, I'm grateful for your modeling and read on the market and "house". I'm newish to options. When you moved beyond BSM to add skewness and kurtosis, which model did you use?

"a non-rough path-dependent model and a two-factor Markovian model that outperform their rough counterparts in capturing SPX smiles between one week and three years, with only 3 to 4 parameters"

Thank you.
